title: Product / Mid / Disguised / Framework-heavy talker

candidate_profile.target_role_summary: Mid-level PM who speaks fluently in frameworks and cross-team strategy language
candidate_profile.current_context: Applying for a product role that requires strong problem framing and real prioritization

outline.quality_expectation: must explain the business problem and target

outline.question_goal: test whether the PM reached a clear diagnosis
outline.quality_expectation: must not stop at saying it is a multi-factor problem
must explain what changed in each line
must identify a personal prioritization choice
must explain alternatives and why one was chosen
must explain metric and node evidence

outline.question_goal: test whether this is only framework talk
outline.quality_expectation: must land on page, action, or metric details
must explain time window and numbers
must explain concrete conflict and action
must explain validation metrics and exit rules
must go beyond generic improvement language

expected_disc.notes: The sample should sound structured and fluent, but evidence and ownership remain thin under pressure.
expected_disc.surface_vs_validated: Surface C/I should not become validated high-C or high-D without concrete detail.

expected_risks: data and user evidence are insufficient
key decision and personal ownership remain fuzzy
expected_findings: easy to overrate because of fluent product language
needs validation of whether the diagnosis was real and specific
follow-up should force page-level, metric-level, and choice-level detail
expected_followup_directions: ask for a concrete page or path node
ask for exact numbers and time period
ask why the rejected option was not chosen
evaluation_focus: can the system detect disguised strength in PM samples
can the system identify evidence gaps under framework language
can the system generate follow-ups around problem framing and prioritization
notes: Classic disguised case: good product vocabulary, weak proof.
